Food safety: raw fish is NOT safe to eat during pregnancy and should be avoided throughout all trimesters. Details: may contain parasites or listeria harmful to fetus.
Food safety: sushi is NOT safe to eat during pregnancy and should be avoided throughout all trimesters. Details: raw fish risk of listeria and parasites.
Food safety: tuna should be consumed in limited amounts during pregnancy throughout all trimesters. Details: high mercury content limit to once per week.
Food safety: salmon cooked is safe to eat during pregnancy throughout all trimesters. Details: excellent omega-3 source for brain development.
Food safety: eggs cooked is safe to eat during pregnancy throughout all trimesters. Details: good protein and choline source.
Food safety: eggs raw is NOT safe to eat during pregnancy and should be avoided throughout all trimesters. Details: risk of salmonella avoid raw or undercooked eggs.
Food safety: soft cheese is NOT safe to eat during pregnancy and should be avoided throughout all trimesters. Details: unpasteurized cheese risk of listeria.
Food safety: hard cheese is safe to eat during pregnancy throughout all trimesters. Details: safe good calcium source.
Food safety: milk pasteurized is safe to eat during pregnancy throughout all trimesters. Details: excellent calcium and protein source.
Food safety: milk unpasteurized is NOT safe to eat during pregnancy and should be avoided throughout all trimesters. Details: risk of listeria and harmful bacteria.
Food safety: leafy greens is safe to eat during pregnancy throughout all trimesters. Details: rich in folate essential for neural tube development.
Food safety: spinach is safe to eat during pregnancy throughout all trimesters. Details: high folate iron and calcium.
Food safety: broccoli is safe to eat during pregnancy throughout all trimesters. Details: rich in vitamins C and K folate.
Food safety: liver should be consumed in limited amounts during pregnancy in trimester first. Details: high vitamin A excess can cause birth defects limit intake.
Food safety: liver is NOT safe to eat during pregnancy and should be avoided in trimester first. Details: very high retinol can cause fetal abnormalities.
Food safety: deli meats is NOT safe to eat during pregnancy and should be avoided throughout all trimesters. Details: risk of listeria reheat until steaming before eating.
Food safety: hot dogs should be consumed in limited amounts during pregnancy throughout all trimesters. Details: reheat thoroughly to avoid listeria risk.
Food safety: caffeine should be consumed in limited amounts during pregnancy throughout all trimesters. Details: limit to 200mg per day high caffeine linked to low birth weight.
Food safety: alcohol is NOT safe to eat during pregnancy and should be avoided throughout all trimesters. Details: no safe level of alcohol during pregnancy.
Food safety: peanuts is safe to eat during pregnancy throughout all trimesters. Details: safe unless allergy no evidence it causes allergy in baby.
Food safety: walnuts is safe to eat during pregnancy throughout all trimesters. Details: rich in omega-3 fatty acids good for brain development.
Food safety: almonds is safe to eat during pregnancy throughout all trimesters. Details: good source of vitamin E and magnesium.
Food safety: berries is safe to eat during pregnancy throughout all trimesters. Details: rich in antioxidants vitamin C and fiber.
Food safety: oranges is safe to eat during pregnancy throughout all trimesters. Details: excellent vitamin C helps iron absorption.
Food safety: bananas is safe to eat during pregnancy throughout all trimesters. Details: good potassium source helps prevent cramps.
Food safety: avocado is safe to eat during pregnancy throughout all trimesters. Details: healthy fats folate and potassium.
Food safety: sweet potato is safe to eat during pregnancy throughout all trimesters. Details: rich in beta-carotene vitamin A source.
Food safety: whole grains is safe to eat during pregnancy throughout all trimesters. Details: fiber and B vitamins important for digestion.
Food safety: lentils is safe to eat during pregnancy throughout all trimesters. Details: excellent plant-based protein and folate.
Food safety: beans is safe to eat during pregnancy throughout all trimesters. Details: good iron fiber and protein source.
Food safety: greek yogurt is safe to eat during pregnancy throughout all trimesters. Details: high protein and calcium probiotic benefits.
Food safety: oily fish is safe to eat during pregnancy throughout all trimesters. Details: omega-3 DHA important for brain development limit to 2 portions per week.
Food safety: iron rich foods is safe to eat during pregnancy throughout all trimesters. Details: red meat beans spinach prevent anemia.
Food safety: folic acid foods is safe to eat during pregnancy in trimester first. Details: leafy greens fortified cereals crucial in first trimester.
Food safety: calcium rich foods is safe to eat during pregnancy throughout all trimesters. Details: dairy broccoli almonds essential for bone development.
Food safety: herbal tea should be consumed in limited amounts during pregnancy throughout all trimesters. Details: some herbs unsafe check with doctor chamomile peppermint generally ok.
Food safety: ginger is safe to eat during pregnancy throughout all trimesters. Details: can help with nausea safe in food amounts.
Food safety: licorice is NOT safe to eat during pregnancy and should be avoided throughout all trimesters. Details: large amounts linked to preterm birth and cognitive issues.
Food safety: papaya unripe is NOT safe to eat during pregnancy and should be avoided throughout all trimesters. Details: contains latex may trigger contractions.
Food safety: pineapple large amounts should be consumed in limited amounts during pregnancy throughout all trimesters. Details: bromelain in large amounts may soften cervix.
Food safety: water is safe to eat during pregnancy throughout all trimesters. Details: stay well hydrated 8-10 glasses per day.
Exercise guidance: walking is safe and recommended during pregnancy throughout all trimesters. Details: recommended light exercise 30 minutes most days safe throughout pregnancy.
Exercise guidance: swimming is safe and recommended during pregnancy throughout all trimesters. Details: excellent low-impact exercise supports body weight gentle on joints.
Exercise guidance: prenatal yoga is safe and recommended during pregnancy throughout all trimesters. Details: improves flexibility reduces stress avoid hot yoga.
Exercise guidance: stationary cycling is safe and recommended during pregnancy throughout all trimesters. Details: low impact good cardio avoid outdoor cycling in later trimesters.
Exercise guidance: low impact aerobics is safe and recommended during pregnancy throughout all trimesters. Details: prenatal aerobics class recommended.
Exercise guidance: kegel exercises is safe and recommended during pregnancy throughout all trimesters. Details: strengthens pelvic floor muscles important for labor and recovery.
Exercise guidance: stretching is safe and recommended during pregnancy throughout all trimesters. Details: gentle stretching reduces muscle tension avoid overstretching.
Exercise guidance: dancing is safe and recommended during pregnancy throughout all trimesters. Details: light dancing safe avoid jumping and sudden direction changes.
Exercise guidance: water aerobics is safe and recommended during pregnancy throughout all trimesters. Details: excellent low-impact reduces swelling and joint pain.
Exercise guidance: pelvic tilts is safe and recommended during pregnancy throughout all trimesters. Details: relieves lower back pain common in pregnancy.
Exercise guidance: cat cow stretch is safe and recommended during pregnancy throughout all trimesters. Details: yoga pose relieves back pain safe throughout pregnancy.
Exercise guidance: side lying exercises is safe and recommended during pregnancy throughout all trimesters. Details: good position for exercise especially in third trimester.
Exercise guidance: heavy lifting is NOT safe during pregnancy and should be avoided throughout all trimesters. Details: risk of injury strain on joints and back avoid.
Exercise guidance: high impact sports is NOT safe during pregnancy and should be avoided throughout all trimesters. Details: jumping running fast risk of falls and abdominal trauma.
Exercise guidance: contact sports is NOT safe during pregnancy and should be avoided throughout all trimesters. Details: boxing martial arts risk of abdominal injury.
Exercise guidance: hot yoga is NOT safe during pregnancy and should be avoided throughout all trimesters. Details: overheating dangerous for fetus avoid all heated exercise classes.
Exercise guidance: scuba diving is NOT safe during pregnancy and should be avoided throughout all trimesters. Details: decompression risk harmful to fetus.
Exercise guidance: horseback riding is NOT safe during pregnancy and should be avoided throughout all trimesters. Details: risk of falls and abdominal trauma.
Exercise guidance: skiing is NOT safe during pregnancy and should be avoided throughout all trimesters. Details: risk of falls avoid especially in second and third trimester.
Exercise guidance: cycling outdoor may be safe with modifications during pregnancy for trimester 2-3. Details: increased fall risk as belly grows switch to stationary bike.
Exercise guidance: running is safe and recommended during pregnancy for trimester 1. Details: safe in first trimester if already a runner reduce intensity as pregnancy progresses.
Exercise guidance: running may be safe with modifications during pregnancy for trimester 2-3. Details: can continue with modifications listen to body reduce pace.
Exercise guidance: abdominal crunches may be safe with modifications during pregnancy throughout all trimesters. Details: avoid in second and third trimester as belly grows.
Exercise guidance: lying flat exercises is NOT safe during pregnancy and should be avoided for trimester 2-3. Details: avoid lying flat on back after first trimester reduces blood flow.
Exercise guidance: core exercises is safe and recommended during pregnancy for trimester 1. Details: safe with modifications avoid intense core work later.
Exercise guidance: pelvic floor exercises is safe and recommended during pregnancy throughout all trimesters. Details: highly recommended throughout pregnancy.
Exercise guidance: strength training light is safe and recommended during pregnancy throughout all trimesters. Details: light weights safe avoid heavy lifting maintain good form.
Exercise guidance: squats is safe and recommended during pregnancy throughout all trimesters. Details: strengthens legs and hips beneficial for labor preparation.
Exercise guidance: breathing exercises is safe and recommended during pregnancy throughout all trimesters. Details: promotes relaxation reduces anxiety prepares for labor.
Pregnancy symptom: nausea. Risk level: LOW. Medical guidance: common in first trimester eat small frequent meals ginger tea may help.
Pregnancy symptom: morning sickness. Risk level: LOW. Medical guidance: normal in first trimester stay hydrated eat bland foods.
Pregnancy symptom: mild fatigue. Risk level: LOW. Medical guidance: normal throughout pregnancy rest when needed iron levels may need checking.
Pregnancy symptom: mild back pain. Risk level: LOW. Medical guidance: common use proper posture gentle exercise warm compress.
Pregnancy symptom: round ligament pain. Risk level: LOW. Medical guidance: normal sharp brief pain in lower abdomen from stretching ligaments.
Pregnancy symptom: braxton hicks. Risk level: LOW. Medical guidance: practice contractions in third trimester normal if not regular or painful.
Pregnancy symptom: heartburn. Risk level: LOW. Medical guidance: common in second and third trimester eat smaller meals avoid spicy food.
Pregnancy symptom: constipation. Risk level: LOW. Medical guidance: common increase fiber and water intake gentle exercise helps.
Pregnancy symptom: mild swelling feet. Risk level: LOW. Medical guidance: normal in third trimester elevate feet rest avoid prolonged standing.
Pregnancy symptom: frequent urination. Risk level: LOW. Medical guidance: normal especially in first and third trimester stay hydrated.
Pregnancy symptom: mood swings. Risk level: LOW. Medical guidance: normal hormonal changes rest mindfulness discuss concerns with midwife.
Pregnancy symptom: food cravings. Risk level: LOW. Medical guidance: normal manage with healthy alternatives.
Pregnancy symptom: mild headache. Risk level: LOW. Medical guidance: rest hydrate avoid known triggers consult doctor if persistent.
Pregnancy symptom: breast tenderness. Risk level: LOW. Medical guidance: normal especially in first trimester supportive bra may help.
Pregnancy symptom: shortness of breath mild. Risk level: LOW. Medical guidance: common in third trimester fetus pressing on diaphragm.
Pregnancy symptom: dizziness mild. Risk level: MEDIUM. Medical guidance: may indicate low blood pressure low blood sugar eat small meals sit slowly.
Pregnancy symptom: severe headache. Risk level: MEDIUM. Medical guidance: could indicate preeclampsia consult doctor promptly.
Pregnancy symptom: severe headache with vision. Risk level: HIGH. Medical guidance: emergency symptom of preeclampsia seek immediate medical help.
Pregnancy symptom: vision changes. Risk level: HIGH. Medical guidance: blurred vision flashing lights spots signs of preeclampsia call doctor immediately.
Pregnancy symptom: severe swelling. Risk level: HIGH. Medical guidance: sudden swelling face hands may indicate preeclampsia seek immediate care.
Pregnancy symptom: swelling one leg. Risk level: HIGH. Medical guidance: could indicate blood clot deep vein thrombosis seek medical attention.
Pregnancy symptom: vaginal bleeding. Risk level: HIGH. Medical guidance: any bleeding requires immediate medical evaluation call doctor or emergency.
Pregnancy symptom: heavy bleeding. Risk level: HIGH. Medical guidance: emergency go to hospital immediately.
Pregnancy symptom: spotting first trimester. Risk level: MEDIUM. Medical guidance: can be normal but must be evaluated by doctor contact healthcare provider.
Pregnancy symptom: regular contractions early. Risk level: HIGH. Medical guidance: before 37 weeks may indicate preterm labor contact doctor immediately.
Pregnancy symptom: decreased fetal movement. Risk level: HIGH. Medical guidance: after 28 weeks fewer than 10 movements in 2 hours contact doctor.
Pregnancy symptom: no fetal movement. Risk level: HIGH. Medical guidance: emergency contact healthcare provider immediately.
Pregnancy symptom: severe abdominal pain. Risk level: HIGH. Medical guidance: could indicate placental abruption ectopic pregnancy seek emergency care.
Pregnancy symptom: persistent vomiting. Risk level: HIGH. Medical guidance: hyperemesis gravidarum risk of dehydration malnutrition contact doctor.
Pregnancy symptom: high fever. Risk level: HIGH. Medical guidance: fever above 38C contact doctor may indicate infection harmful to fetus.
Pregnancy symptom: fever. Risk level: MEDIUM. Medical guidance: temperature above 37.5C rest fluids contact doctor if persistent or high.
Pregnancy symptom: urinary burning. Risk level: MEDIUM. Medical guidance: possible urinary tract infection UTI common in pregnancy contact doctor.
Pregnancy symptom: painful urination. Risk level: MEDIUM. Medical guidance: could be UTI requires antibiotic treatment safe for pregnancy contact doctor.
Pregnancy symptom: leaking fluid. Risk level: HIGH. Medical guidance: may indicate water breaking premature rupture of membranes contact doctor.
Pregnancy symptom: watery discharge. Risk level: HIGH. Medical guidance: if constant could be amniotic fluid leaking seek immediate medical evaluation.
Pregnancy symptom: itching severe. Risk level: MEDIUM. Medical guidance: severe itching especially palms soles may indicate cholestasis contact doctor.
Pregnancy symptom: jaundice. Risk level: HIGH. Medical guidance: yellowing of skin eyes contact doctor immediately.
Pregnancy symptom: chest pain. Risk level: HIGH. Medical guidance: could indicate cardiac issue or pulmonary embolism seek emergency care.
Pregnancy symptom: difficulty breathing. Risk level: HIGH. Medical guidance: severe shortness of breath seek emergency medical care.
Pregnancy symptom: rapid weight gain. Risk level: MEDIUM. Medical guidance: sudden large weight gain may indicate preeclampsia contact doctor.
Pregnancy symptom: no weight gain. Risk level: MEDIUM. Medical guidance: inadequate weight gain consult doctor and nutritionist.
Pregnancy symptom: depression. Risk level: MEDIUM. Medical guidance: prenatal depression common but requires support contact healthcare provider mental health support.
Pregnancy symptom: anxiety severe. Risk level: MEDIUM. Medical guidance: severe anxiety discuss with healthcare provider safe therapies available.

Pregnancy week 4 (Trimester 1): Baby development: embryo implanted neural tube forming. Nutrition advice: start folic acid 400mcg daily avoid alcohol. Exercise advice: gentle walking only avoid strenuous activity.

Pregnancy week 5 (Trimester 1): Baby development: heart begins to beat embryo size of apple seed. Nutrition advice: continue folic acid increase vitamin B6 for nausea. Exercise advice: light walking rest when tired.

Pregnancy week 6 (Trimester 1): Baby development: facial features forming brain developing. Nutrition advice: eat small frequent meals for nausea ginger tea helps. Exercise advice: gentle walks rest as needed fatigue is normal.

Pregnancy week 7 (Trimester 1): Baby development: limb buds appearing kidneys forming. Nutrition advice: stay hydrated eat iron-rich foods leafy greens. Exercise advice: light activity as tolerated morning sickness may limit exercise.

Pregnancy week 8 (Trimester 1): Baby development: fingers and toes forming all major organs present. Nutrition advice: folate iron calcium are crucial prenatal vitamins important. Exercise advice: gentle yoga or walking rest often.

Pregnancy week 9 (Trimester 1): Baby development: baby size of a grape facial features more defined. Nutrition advice: eat protein with each meal helps stabilize blood sugar. Exercise advice: light walks swimming if tolerated.

Pregnancy week 10 (Trimester 1): Baby development: baby size of a strawberry reproductive organs forming. Nutrition advice: increase omega-3 intake oily fish walnuts. Exercise advice: gentle exercise 20-30 minutes most days.

Pregnancy week 11 (Trimester 1): Baby development: baby size of a fig fingers and toes separated. Nutrition advice: continue iron-rich foods risk of anemia increases. Exercise advice: gentle yoga walking swimming.

Pregnancy week 12 (Trimester 1): Baby development: baby size of a lime reflexes developing. Nutrition advice: maintain balanced diet nausea often improves this week. Exercise advice: can increase activity as energy returns.

Pregnancy week 13 (Trimester 1): Baby development: end of first trimester baby size of a lemon. Nutrition advice: celebrate end of highest risk period continue prenatal vitamins. Exercise advice: activity generally increases as nausea reduces.

Pregnancy week 14 (Trimester 2): Baby development: baby can make facial expressions size of a peach. Nutrition advice: increase calorie intake by 300 calories per day. Exercise advice: walking swimming prenatal yoga all recommended.

Pregnancy week 15 (Trimester 2): Baby development: baby forming taste buds size of an apple. Nutrition advice: eat calcium-rich foods dairy broccoli almonds for bone development. Exercise advice: 30 minutes moderate exercise most days.

Pregnancy week 16 (Trimester 2): Baby development: baby making sucking movements size of an avocado. Nutrition advice: iron intake important red meat beans spinach prevent anemia. Exercise advice: swimming excellent as belly grows low impact.

Pregnancy week 17 (Trimester 2): Baby development: baby developing fat stores size of a pear. Nutrition advice: vitamin D important for calcium absorption dairy fortified foods. Exercise advice: prenatal yoga and walking excellent.

Pregnancy week 18 (Trimester 2): Baby development: baby can hear sounds size of a bell pepper. Nutrition advice: increase protein for fetal growth meat eggs legumes. Exercise advice: walking swimming and prenatal yoga recommended.

Pregnancy week 19 (Trimester 2): Baby development: baby covered in vernix size of a mango. Nutrition advice: omega-3 DHA critical for brain development oily fish walnuts. Exercise advice: continue regular moderate exercise.

Pregnancy week 20 (Trimester 2): Baby development: halfway point baby size of a banana. Nutrition advice: calcium needs increase 1000mg daily for skeleton development. Exercise advice: water aerobics excellent for growing belly.

Pregnancy week 21 (Trimester 2): Baby development: baby has eyebrows size of a carrot. Nutrition advice: iron-rich foods essential anemia risk increases in second trimester. Exercise advice: swimming walking light strength training.

Pregnancy week 22 (Trimester 2): Baby development: baby developing sleep cycles size of a papaya. Nutrition advice: zinc and magnesium important for healthy development wholegrains nuts. Exercise advice: prenatal yoga excellent for back pain and flexibility.

Pregnancy week 23 (Trimester 2): Baby development: baby fingerprints forming size of a large mango. Nutrition advice: continue DHA and iron rich diet. Exercise advice: moderate walking swimming continue.

Pregnancy week 24 (Trimester 2): Baby development: viability milestone baby size of an ear of corn. Nutrition advice: increase fiber intake to prevent constipation whole grains vegetables. Exercise advice: pelvic floor exercises daily swimming walking.

Pregnancy week 25 (Trimester 2): Baby development: baby growing fat rapidly size of a rutabaga. Nutrition advice: protein needs highest now 70-100g daily meat dairy legumes. Exercise advice: continue moderate exercise adjust intensity as needed.

Pregnancy week 26 (Trimester 2): Baby development: baby opening eyes size of a scallion. Nutrition advice: calcium and vitamin D for bone development continue prenatal vitamins. Exercise advice: water aerobics walking prenatal yoga.

Pregnancy week 27 (Trimester 3): Baby development: end of second trimester baby size of a cauliflower. Nutrition advice: omega-3 for brain development in third trimester. Exercise advice: continue appropriate exercise intensity may need to reduce.

Pregnancy week 28 (Trimester 3): Baby development: baby can dream REM sleep developing. Nutrition advice: iron and vitamin C together enhance absorption. Exercise advice: pelvic floor exercises swimming walking.

Pregnancy week 29 (Trimester 3): Baby development: baby head may be engaging size of a butternut squash. Nutrition advice: small frequent meals as stomach compressed by uterus. Exercise advice: gentle walking swimming pelvic tilts for back pain.

Pregnancy week 30 (Trimester 3): Baby development: baby storing iron and calcium. Nutrition advice: avoid large meals heartburn common eat small portions. Exercise advice: walking swimming prenatal yoga for labor preparation.

Pregnancy week 31 (Trimester 3): Baby development: baby putting on weight rapidly. Nutrition advice: increase calcium 1200mg daily for final bone development. Exercise advice: gentle activity focus on pelvic floor and breathing.

Pregnancy week 32 (Trimester 3): Baby development: baby practicing breathing movements. Nutrition advice: eat iron-rich foods to prepare for blood loss in labor. Exercise advice: walking and swimming remain excellent avoid lying flat.

Pregnancy week 33 (Trimester 3): Baby development: baby skeleton hardening except skull. Nutrition advice: vitamin K important in late pregnancy leafy greens. Exercise advice: gentle exercise walking pelvic floor exercises.

Pregnancy week 34 (Trimester 3): Baby development: baby immune system strengthening. Nutrition advice: continue DHA omega-3 for final brain development. Exercise advice: walking pelvic tilts cat-cow stretches for back comfort.

Pregnancy week 35 (Trimester 3): Baby development: baby head down in most cases. Nutrition advice: eat small meals frequently to manage compression. Exercise advice: gentle walks breathing exercises prepare for labor.

Pregnancy week 36 (Trimester 3): Baby development: baby considered late preterm almost full term. Nutrition advice: maintain nutrition prepare hospital bag. Exercise advice: gentle walking pelvic floor exercises labor preparation breathing.

Pregnancy week 37 (Trimester 3): Baby development: baby full term lungs mature. Nutrition advice: continue balanced nutrition hydration important. Exercise advice: gentle walks pelvic floor exercises rest as needed.

Pregnancy week 38 (Trimester 3): Baby development: baby ready for birth. Nutrition advice: eat iron-rich foods maintain hydration. Exercise advice: light walking pelvic floor exercises rest when needed.
Pregnancy week 39 (Trimester 3): Baby development: baby gaining weight slowing. Nutrition advice: maintain balanced diet. Exercise advice: gentle walks rest as needed body preparing for labor.

Pregnancy week 40 (Trimester 3): Baby development: full term expected due date week. Nutrition advice: stay hydrated eat iron-rich foods. Exercise advice: gentle walks rest body preparing for labor birth.
